extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won in a chaotic sprint finish, returning to victory nearly three years after his ban from the JAKOBSEN crash, beating VAN AERT and PHILIPSEN in a bike throw.
tactical_patterns: CORT Magnus attacked solo at race start to secure uncontested KOM point, preempting breakaway competition for polka dot jersey control.
GROENEWEGEN Dylan positioned left of SAGAN Peter after mid-race crash, exploiting barrier pinch that caught EWAN Caleb on right.
VAN AERT Wout moved over on SAGAN Peter in sprint, potentially illegal deviation risking green jersey disqualification despite second place.
JAKOBSEN Fabio finished fifth after being caught in 10km crash, suggesting adrenaline surge from crash recovery aids sprint performance.

rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: Won first Tour stage since 9-month ban, showed elite bike throw timing and positioning instincts despite earlier crash involvement at 10km.
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: Second place three consecutive stages reveals pattern of poor final positioning or timing despite clear speed advantage.
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: positive: Won all six available KOM points across two stages via solo early attacks, showing tactical dominance in category.
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: Third place suggests Alpecin sprint train competitive despite JAKOBSEN crash involvement and VAN DER POEL domestique duties.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Fourth after being moved over by VAN AERT, showing diminished ability to hold position against physical sprinters.
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: neutral: Fifth after 10km crash suggests crash recovery adrenaline helped performance but positioning still suboptimal.
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: Pinched on barriers on right side while GROENEWEGEN went left, poor positioning instinct in chaotic finale.
